touches.ai_summary: Isabella Yoon submitted an inbound demo request via website form. Interested in call recording and coaching tools for their CS team. Currently evaluating alternatives including Fireflies.
Follow-up email sent with calendar link and preliminary questions about team size and current workflow. Isabella responded confirming 20-seat requirement and interest in automation features.

questions_asked.question: How many team members would need access?

touches.ai_summary: Sent discovery call confirmation with agenda. Isabella confirmed availability and mentioned they're looking to implement before Q2 planning cycle.
next_steps: Discovery call scheduled for next Tuesday

touches.ai_summary: Pre-call email with discovery framework and use case examples in PropTech. Isabella acknowledged and shared they're comparing against Fireflies based on peer recommendation.

questions_asked.question: Do you integrate with Salesforce and Zendesk?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, native integrations for both platforms

touches.ai_summary: Discovery call with Isabella covered current pain points: inconsistent call documentation, lack of coaching insights, manual CRM updates. Team of 20 (12 CS, 8 Sales) needs recording, transcription, and sentiment analysis. Budget around $20K annually. Fireflies is primary alternative but lacks automation they need.

questions_asked.question: What makes your automation different from Fireflies?
questions_asked.answer: We offer workflow triggers and custom playbooks, not just transcription

questions_asked.question: Who else should be involved in evaluating this?
questions_asked.answer: Ade in RevOps will handle technical eval, Megan in Sales for procurement

next_steps: Loop in Ade for technical questions

touches.ai_summary: Sent discovery recap with pricing overview for 20-seat unlimited automation plan ($19,600 ACV). Included ROI calculator and PropTech case study. Isabella responded positively, requested demo with focus on coaching workflows.
next_steps: Schedule demo with Isabella and team

touches.ai_summary: Demo scheduling email with proposed times. Isabella confirmed Thursday works and will bring Ade from RevOps. Requested focus on Salesforce integration and custom playbook setup.

questions_asked.question: Can we see the coaching dashboard during the demo?
questions_asked.answer: Absolutely, we'll walk through the full coaching workflow

next_steps: Demo scheduled for Thursday 3pm with Isabella and Ade

touches.ai_summary: Pre-demo alignment call to confirm use cases and attendees. Isabella mentioned leadership is supportive but hasn't been directly involved yet. Confirmed Ade will evaluate integration complexity and Megan will join later for commercial discussions.

questions_asked.question: Should we plan an exec briefing with your VP or Director?
questions_asked.answer: Maybe later—let's get through technical eval first

touches.ai_summary: Product demo with Isabella covering call recording, auto-transcription, sentiment tagging, and coaching playbooks. Walked through Salesforce sync and custom automation workflows. Isabella engaged throughout, liked coaching features and automation depth compared to Fireflies.

questions_asked.question: How long does implementation typically take?
questions_asked.answer: 2-3 weeks for standard setup with Salesforce and Zendesk

questions_asked.question: Can we customize the sentiment model for our industry?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we offer custom training for enterprise plans

next_steps: Send demo recording and feature comparison vs Fireflies

touches.ai_summary: Technical evaluation call with Ade Thomas. Reviewed API documentation, Salesforce field mapping, data retention policies, and security compliance (SOC 2). Ade asked detailed questions about webhook reliability and custom object support. Positive technical feedback but noted need for internal security review.

questions_asked.question: Do you support custom Salesforce objects or just standard?
questions_asked.answer: Both—we can map to any custom object with API access

questions_asked.question: What's your data residency policy for EU customers?
questions_asked.answer: US-based storage standard, EU residency available on request

objections: Need to run this through InfoSec for compliance approval
next_steps: Send security documentation and SOC 2 report
Intro to Solutions Engineer for integration planning

touches.ai_summary: Proposal review call with Isabella. Presented $19,600/year for 20 seats with unlimited automation. Isabella receptive to pricing but mentioned Fireflies quoted significantly lower. Discussed ROI based on coaching time saved and CRM automation. Isabella to review internally with Megan.

questions_asked.question: Can you break down what's included in unlimited automation vs your lower tiers?
questions_asked.answer: Unlimited includes custom playbooks, workflow triggers, and API access—lower tiers cap automations at 10/month

objections: Fireflies pricing is about 40% lower for similar seat count
next_steps: Schedule follow-up with Megan for procurement alignment

touches.ai_summary: Sent proposal document with detailed feature comparison against Fireflies, highlighting automation capabilities, coaching workflows, and implementation support. Isabella acknowledged receipt and said she's meeting with Megan and Ade this week to discuss next steps.
next_steps: Follow up after internal review meeting

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email checking on internal review progress. Isabella responded that team is aligned on value but pricing gap with Fireflies is a sticking point. Asked if there's flexibility on per-seat cost or contract length.

questions_asked.question: Would a multi-year commitment reduce the annual cost?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we can explore 10-15% discount for 2-3 year terms

next_steps: Prepare pricing scenarios for multi-year terms

touches.ai_summary: Executive alignment call with Isabella. Discussed strategic value and implementation timeline. Isabella mentioned her VP is supportive in principle but not yet directly engaged. Covered ROI case and competitive differentiation. Isabella wants to finalize pricing before bringing VP into formal approval.

questions_asked.question: What would make this a no-brainer for your VP to approve?
questions_asked.answer: Closing the pricing gap or showing hard ROI on coaching efficiency

next_steps: Refine ROI model with Isabella's metrics

touches.ai_summary: Proposal refinement call. Reviewed updated ROI calculator showing 150+ hours saved quarterly on coaching and CRM updates. Isabella engaged and supportive. Confirmed Megan will drive final commercial terms and contract review once pricing is settled.

questions_asked.question: If we sign before end of quarter, is there any expedited onboarding available?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we can prioritize implementation to go live within 2 weeks

next_steps: Connect with Megan on contract terms

touches.ai_summary: Pricing call with Isabella and Megan. Discussed revised proposal at $17,640 for 2-year term (10% discount). Megan asked about payment terms and auto-renewal clauses. Isabella supportive of moving forward at this price point. Megan to review contract template with legal.

questions_asked.question: Can we do annual payments instead of upfront?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we offer annual billing with no additional cost

questions_asked.question: What's the expansion pricing if we add seats mid-contract?
questions_asked.answer: Prorated at same per-seat rate for remainder of term

objections: Need to remove auto-renewal clause for internal policy compliance
next_steps: Send updated contract with annual billing and no auto-renewal

touches.ai_summary: Sent revised contract reflecting annual billing and no auto-renewal. Isabella confirmed received and forwarded to Megan and legal team. Expected 1-week turnaround for contract review.
next_steps: Check in mid-week on legal review status

touches.ai_summary: Executive alignment meeting with Isabella to discuss implementation readiness and confirm internal approval process. Isabella mentioned legal has minor redlines on liability caps. Also noted VP still not directly involved but has delegated approval authority to her and Megan. Discussed go-live timeline for early Q2.

questions_asked.question: Are there any other stakeholders we should connect with before signing?
questions_asked.answer: No, Megan and I have authority to finalize this

touches.ai_summary: Final alignment call with Isabella and Megan. Reviewed legal redlines—requested liability cap increase to $100K. Discussed mutual NDA and data processing addendum. Both confirmed readiness to sign once legal terms settled. Isabella optimistic about closing this week.

questions_asked.question: Can we get dedicated CSM assigned before go-live?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we'll assign CSM within 48 hours of contract signature

objections: Liability cap at $50K is below our standard—need $100K minimum
next_steps: Escalate liability cap request to legal team

touches.ai_summary: Negotiation call to finalize contract terms. Agreed to $100K liability cap and mutual NDA. Megan confirmed procurement approval and Isabella confirmed technical readiness with Ade. Discussed implementation kickoff schedule for week of April 22nd. Both parties aligned on moving to signature.

questions_asked.question: What's the cancellation policy if we need to terminate early?
questions_asked.answer: 30-day notice required, no early termination fee for cause

touches.ai_summary: Sent final contract via DocuSign to Isabella and Megan for signature. Included implementation kickoff invite for April 22nd with Ade and implementation team. Isabella confirmed she'll review and sign by end of week.
